title: Data storage system and magnetic tape recording media
abstract: An apparatus according to one approach includes an array of skew detection transducers. An array of write transducers is spaced from the array of skew detection transducers along an intended direction of tape travel thereacross. An array of read transducers is aligned with the array of write transducers in the intended direction of tape travel. The apparatus also includes a first actuator configured to operatively exert a force on the array of skew detection transducers for orienting a longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers substantially orthogonal to the actual direction of tape travel across the array of skew detection transducers. A magnetic recording medium according to one approach includes a magnetic recording tape having a longitudinal axis extending between distal ends thereof, the magnetic recording tape having vertical bars written in servo-skew patterns thereof, the vertical bars being oriented perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the tape.
claims:
Claims 35 CLAIMS 1. An apparatus comprising: an array of skew detection transducers; an array of write transducers spaced from the array of skew detection transducers along an intended direction of tape travel thereacross; an array of read transducers aligned with the array of write transducers in the intended direction of tape travel; and a first actuator configured to operatively exert a force on the array of skew detection transducers for orienting a longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers substantially orthogonal to the actual direction of tape travel across the array of skew detection transducers. 2. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein longitudinal axes of the arrays of read and write transducers are not pivotable from an orientation thereof relative to the longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers, wherein the first actuator is configured to move the first and the at least one second module together. 3. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the array of skew detection transducers is on a first module, wherein the arrays of read and write transducers are on at least one second module. 4. The apparatus as claimed in claim 3, wherein the at least one second module is movable relative to the first module, wherein the first actuator is configured to move the first and the at least one second module together; and comprising a second actuator configured to exert a force on the at least one second module for causing a relative movement of the at least one second module with respect to the first module for adjusting a tilt angle of the at least one second module in response to a determined state of lateral expansion of the tape. 5. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein longitudinal axes of the arrays of read and write transducers are nominally tilted by greater than 0Â° from the longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers. 6. The apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , comprising a controller configured to control the first actuator to compensate for tape skew based on readback signals from the skew detection transducers. 7. The apparatus as claimed in claim 6, comprising servo readers flanking the array of write transducers; servo readers flanking the array of read transducers; and a controller configured to control actuation of the arrays of read and write transducers for track following based on a readback signal from the servo readers. 

 36 8. A data storage system, comprising: a magnetic head having the arrays as recited in claim 6; a drive mechanism for passing a magnetic tape over the magnetic head; and the controller. 9. The apparatus as claimed in claim 6, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading vertical bars of a timing-based servo pattern having chevrons and the vertical bars. 10. The apparatus as claimed in claim 6, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading chevrons of a timing-based servo pattern. 11. An apparatus comprising: a first module having an array of skew detection transducers; an array of write transducers spaced from the array of skew detection transducers along an intended direction of tape travel thereacross; an array of read transducers aligned with the array of write transducers in the intended direction of tape travel, wherein the arrays of read and write transducers are on at least one second module, wherein the at least one second module is movable relative to the first module; a first actuator configured to move the first and the at least one second module together; and a second actuator configured to exert a force on the at least one second module for causing a relative movement of the at least one second module with respect to the first module for adjusting a tilt angle of the at least one second module in response to a determined state of lateral expansion of the tape. 12. The apparatus as claimed in claim 11 , wherein longitudinal axes of the arrays of read and write transducers are nominally tilted by greater than 0Â° from the longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers. 13. The apparatus as claimed in claim 11 , comprising a controller configured to control the first actuator to compensate for tape skew based on readback signals from the skew detection transducers. 14. The apparatus as claimed in claim 13, comprising servo readers flanking the array of write transducers; servo readers flanking the array of read transducers; and a controller configured to control actuation of the arrays of read and write transducers for track following based on a readback signal from the servo readers. 15. The apparatus as claimed in claim 13, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading vertical bars of a timing-based servo pattern having chevrons and the vertical bars. 16. The apparatus as claimed in claim 13, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading chevrons of a timing-based servo pattern. 17. An apparatus comprising: a first module having an array of skew detection transducers; an array of write transducers spaced from the array of skew detection transducers along an intended direction of tape travel thereacross; an array of read transducers aligned with the array of write transducers in the intended direction of tape travel, wherein the arrays of read and write transducers are on at least one second module, wherein the at least one second module is movable relative to the first module; and a first actuator configured to operatively exert a force on the array of skew detection transducers for maintaining an orientation of a longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers substantially orthogonal to the direction of tape travel, wherein longitudinal axes of the arrays of read and write transducers are not pivotable from an orientation thereof relative to the longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers, wherein the first actuator is configured to move the first and the at least one second module together. 18. The apparatus as claimed in claim 17, wherein longitudinal axes of the arrays of read and write transducers are parallel to the longitudinal axis of the array of skew detection transducers. 19. The apparatus as claimed in claim 17, comprising a controller configured to control the first actuator to compensate for tape skew based on readback signals from the skew detection transducers. 20. The apparatus as claimed in claim 19, comprising servo readers flanking the array of write transducers; servo readers flanking the array of read transducers; and a controller configured to control actuation of the arrays of read and write transducers for track following based on a readback signal from the servo readers. 21 . The apparatus as claimed in claim 19, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading vertical bars of a timing-based servo pattern having chevrons and the vertical bars. 22. The apparatus as claimed in claim 19, wherein the controller is configured to compute a skew correction value based on the readback signals from the skew detection transducers reading chevrons of a timing-based servo pattern. 23. A magnetic recording medium, comprising: a magnetic recording tape having a longitudinal axis extending between distal ends thereof, the magnetic recording tape having vertical bars written in servo-skew patterns thereof, the vertical bars being oriented perpendicular to the longitudinal axis of the magnetic recording tape. 24. The magnetic recording medium as claimed in claim 23, wherein the vertical bars have no other purpose than for skew correction. 25. The magnetic recording medium as claimed in claim 23 or claim 24, wherein the vertical bars are positioned above and/or below chevron-based timing-based servo frames of the servo pattern.
